messages.content:
You are an expert English linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in English with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

en_unspecified_2827 | sense 2 | idiom: from hunger | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Of very poor quality; only acceptable in desperate circumstances or when no better option is available.

en_unspecified_2828 | sense 1 | idiom: from my cold, dead hands | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Only after my death; only when I am literally dead.

en_unspecified_2828 | sense 2 | idiom: from my cold, dead hands | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Under no circumstances; never; an emphatic refusal to give up or surrender something.

en_unspecified_2829 | sense 1 | idiom: from pillar to post | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: From one pillar or post to another; moving from one fixed point to another.

en_unspecified_2829 | sense 2 | idiom: from pillar to post | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Sent or moved about aimlessly or repeatedly between different people, places, or authorities, often implying inconvenience, frustration, or failure to obtain redress.

en_unspecified_2830 | sense 1 | idiom: from post to pillar | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, moved or tossed from one post or pillar to another.

en_unspecified_2830 | sense 2 | idiom: from post to pillar | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively, to be sent repeatedly from one place, person, or authority to another; to be bounced about or passed along without resolution.

en_unspecified_2831 | sense 1 | idiom: from scratch | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: From the beginning; starting anew with no advantage, prior preparation, or preexisting work.

en_unspecified_2831 | sense 2 | idiom: from scratch | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Made or prepared using only basic ingredients or raw materials rather than pre-made components.

en_unspecified_2832 | sense 1 | idiom: from stem to stern | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Along the full length of a vessel, from its bow (stem) to its stern.

en_unspecified_2832 | sense 2 | idiom: from stem to stern | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: From one end to the other; throughout; completely.

en_unspecified_2833 | sense 1 | idiom: from the cradle to the grave | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, from birth (the cradle) until death (the grave).

en_unspecified_2833 | sense 2 | idiom: from the cradle to the grave | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Spanning an entire lifetime; covering all stages of life from birth to death.

en_unspecified_2834 | sense 1 | idiom: from the Department of the Bleeding Obvious | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A sarcastic expression attributing a statement to an imaginary department that issues information so obvious that mentioning it is unnecessary; used to mock the banality or redundancy of an observation.

en_unspecified_2835 | sense 1 | idiom: from the East German judge | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Attributed to or coming from a judge who is (or was) East German; literal attribution of an assessment to a judge from East Germany.

en_unspecified_2835 | sense 2 | idiom: from the East German judge | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A joking, hyperbolic way to indicate an unusually low, stingy, or disparaging score or judgment in a competition or appraisal.

en_unspecified_2836 | sense 1 | idiom: from the gecko | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Originating from or associated with a gecko, the small lizard.

en_unspecified_2836 | sense 2 | idiom: from the gecko | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A nonstandard or erroneous variant of 'from the get-go', used informally to mean 'from the very beginning.'

en_unspecified_2837 | sense 1 | idiom: from the get-go | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: At the moment of initial motion or the precise time when something begins.

en_unspecified_2837 | sense 2 | idiom: from the get-go | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: From the very beginning; from the outset; immediately upon starting.

en_unspecified_2838 | sense 1 | idiom: from the ground up | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Beginning at ground level and proceeding upward; physically starting from the ground.

en_unspecified_2838 | sense 2 | idiom: from the ground up | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Starting from the basics or foundation and building or reconstructing completely; from first principles.

en_unspecified_2839 | sense 1 | idiom: from the rooter to the tooter | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: From head to toe; entirely or throughout the whole of someone or something.

en_unspecified_2839 | sense 2 | idiom: from the rooter to the tooter | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: With minimal waste; making use of all available parts or resources.

en_unspecified_2840 | sense 1 | idiom: from the word go | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: From the very beginning; from the outset; immediately upon starting.

en_unspecified_2840 | sense 2 | idiom: from the word go | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Starting at the point when a signal or command to begin (for example, the word "go") is given.

en_unspecified_2841 | sense 1 | idiom: from time to time | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Occasionally; at intervals; once in a while.

en_unspecified_2841 | sense 2 | idiom: from time to time | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: At various times or as those times occur; in whatever status exists at different times.

en_unspecified_2841 | sense 3 | idiom: from time to time | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Continuously from one time to another; at all times.

en_unspecified_2842 | sense 1 | idiom: from womb to tomb | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Throughout the entirety of a person's life, from birth to death.
